accounting-services.metaTitle: Small Business Accountant | Thryv Cape Town
accounting-services.metaDescription: Looking for a small business accountant in Cape Town? Thryv offers fixed-fee monthly accounting that keeps you compliant and growing. Chat to us today.

body.heading: A small business accountant who actually knows your numbers
paragraphs: When you run a growing business, you need more than someone who files paperwork once a year. As your small business accountant, we get to know how your business actually works, so the advice you get fits your reality, not a generic template. You speak to people who remember your name and your goals.

Our fixed-fee accounting model means no surprise invoices and no clock-watching when you call with a question. You pay one predictable monthly amount and get a full finance function behind you. If you also need day-to-day record keeping, our bookkeeping services slot in neatly alongside your accounting.

body.heading: Fixed-fee accounting services built around SME owners
paragraphs: Most Cape Town and Stellenbosch SME owners do not want to manage a payroll for an in-house finance team. Outsourced accounting gives you senior-level expertise at a fraction of that cost, with our monthly accounting packages scaling as you grow. You get the chartered accountant insight of a larger firm without the overhead.

Beyond the books, we help you read them. Management accounts, cash-flow guidance and growth advice turn raw figures into decisions you can act on. Need tidy income tax and VAT support or compliant annual financial statements too? It is all handled under one fixed monthly fee.

body.heading: Staying SARS-compliant without the stress
paragraphs: SARS deadlines, provisional tax estimates and VAT returns have a way of creeping up. As your accountant, we track these dates for you and prepare submissions accurately and on time, so penalties and late-payment interest stop eating into your margins. You stay focused on running the business.

We work in modern cloud accounting software, which means your numbers are always current and we can spot issues early. Whether you are in Cape Town, Stellenbosch or elsewhere in South Africa, you can reach us easily. Have a look at how getting started with Thryv works whenever you are ready.

faqs.q: How much does a small business accountant cost at Thryv?
faqs.a: We charge one fixed monthly fee rather than hourly rates, so you always know what you are paying. The exact amount depends on your transaction volume, VAT status and the services you need. We agree it upfront, with no surprise invoices, and review it as your business grows.

faqs.q: Do you work with businesses outside Cape Town and Stellenbosch?
faqs.a: Yes. While we are based in Cape Town and Stellenbosch, our cloud-based approach means we comfortably support SME owners across South Africa. Your records stay current online, we meet by video or phone when needed, and you get the same responsive service wherever your business is based.

faqs.q: What is included in your monthly accounting packages?
faqs.a: Each package typically covers monthly bookkeeping and reconciliations, VAT, income and provisional tax, annual financial statements, cloud software and management accounts with growth advice. We tailor the mix to your business so you only pay for what you genuinely need, all under one predictable fee.

faqs.q: Can you take over from my current accountant?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Switching is simpler than most owners expect. With your permission we liaise with your previous accountant, gather your historical records and handle the SARS profile transfer. We manage the admin so the move is smooth and you experience no gap in your compliance or reporting.

faqs.q: Do I still need a bookkeeper if I have an accountant?
faqs.a: It depends on your volume. Some clients use our bookkeeping and accounting together so daily records and higher-level reporting stay joined up. Others handle basic capture in-house while we manage reconciliations, compliance and statements. We will recommend the most cost-effective split for your situation.

bookkeeping-services.metaDescription: Reliable bookkeeping services for Cape Town SMEs. Thryv keeps your books accurate, current and SARS-ready with cloud bookkeeping. Get in touch today.

body.heading: Bookkeeping services that keep you in control
paragraphs: Good decisions start with good records. Our bookkeeping services keep every transaction captured, categorised and reconciled, so you are never guessing about your cash position. Instead of scrambling at year-end, you have clean books you can trust month after month.

Bookkeeping for small business owners should save you time, not create more admin. We take the day-to-day capture off your plate and give it back to you as clear, usable information. When the bigger picture matters, our fixed-fee accounting services build straight on top of the same tidy records.

body.heading: Cloud bookkeeping with real-time visibility
paragraphs: With cloud bookkeeping, your numbers live in one secure place you can check from anywhere. Real-time dashboards show what you are owed, what you owe and how cash is tracking, so a Cape Town or Stellenbosch owner can make confident calls without waiting for a month-end report.

As your outsourced bookkeeper, we manage accounts payable and receivable, bank and credit-card reconciliations and a proper monthly close. Accurate books also make your VAT and tax submissions far smoother, because the source data SARS relies on is already correct and up to date.

body.heading: A bookkeeper in Cape Town who scales with you
paragraphs: Outsourced bookkeeping means you get a skilled team without hiring, training or covering leave. Whether you process a handful of invoices a month or hundreds, we adjust to your volume, and our fixed monthly fee keeps the cost predictable as your business in South Africa grows.

Clean books feed everything else, from payroll to reporting. If you want payslips and PAYE handled too, our payroll services connect neatly to your bookkeeping. Curious how it all fits together? You can get in touch with our Cape Town team for a straightforward chat.

faqs.q: What is the difference between bookkeeping and accounting?
faqs.a: Bookkeeping is the day-to-day work of recording and reconciling transactions, so your data is accurate and current. Accounting builds on that to interpret the numbers, prepare financial statements and advise on tax and growth. At Thryv the two work together seamlessly under one roof.

faqs.q: Which cloud bookkeeping software do you use?
faqs.a: We work with leading cloud platforms such as Xero, which give you secure access and real-time dashboards from any device. We will recommend the best fit for your business, handle the setup and migration, and make sure your team is comfortable using it day to day.

faqs.q: How often will my books be updated?
faqs.a: Because we use cloud bookkeeping, your records are kept current throughout the month rather than only at year-end. We complete a formal monthly close with reconciliations and reporting, so you always have a clear, up-to-date view of your finances when you need to make a decision.

faqs.q: Is outsourced bookkeeping suitable for a very small business?
faqs.a: Yes. Outsourced bookkeeping is often ideal for smaller businesses, because you get professional records without the cost of hiring. Our fixed monthly fee scales to your volume, so even a sole owner or early-stage SME in Cape Town gets accurate, SARS-ready books at a sensible price.

faqs.q: Will accurate bookkeeping help at tax time?
faqs.a: Definitely. Clean, reconciled books are the foundation of stress-free tax. When your records are accurate and current, VAT, provisional and income tax submissions become quicker and far less risky, reducing the chance of errors, SARS queries or penalties. It also means fewer last-minute surprises each year.

tax-services.metaTitle: Tax Services Cape Town & Stellenbosch | Thryv
tax-services.metaDescription: Expert tax services for Cape Town & Stellenbosch SMEs. Stay SARS-compliant on income tax, provisional tax and VAT. Talk to our tax practitioners today.

body.heading: Tax Services Built Around Your Business
paragraphs: SARS rules shift constantly, and one missed deadline can cost you penalties and sleepless nights. Our chartered accountants handle the full picture of tax services for you, from income tax and provisional tax submissions to VAT, so you can get back to running your business with confidence.

We work with small business owners across Cape Town, Stellenbosch and the rest of South Africa. As registered tax practitioners, we file accurately and on time, manage every SARS deadline, and keep you fully compliant. You stay informed in plain language, never buried in jargon.
Our tax work plugs straight into your monthly accounting services, so your numbers, returns and records always line up. One team, one fixed monthly fee, no surprises at year-end.

body.heading: Income Tax, Provisional Tax and VAT, Handled
paragraphs: We prepare and submit your annual income tax returns and both provisional tax submissions, calculating estimates carefully to avoid over- or under-payment penalties. If you trade through a company, we make sure your business and personal tax positions work together sensibly.

Crossed the R1 million turnover threshold, or want the credibility VAT brings? We manage VAT registration in South Africa, prepare your returns and submit them on time, every cycle. Accurate bookkeeping records make this smooth and audit-ready.

Beyond filing, we focus on legally minimising what you owe. Thoughtful tax planning, structured around your real numbers, can free up cash to reinvest in your business rather than hand to SARS unnecessarily.

body.heading: SARS Correspondence, Disputes and Peace of Mind
paragraphs: When SARS sends a letter, a verification request or a query, it lands with us, not on your desk. We respond on your behalf, manage objections and disputes, and resolve issues before they snowball into penalties or interest.
Staying penalty-free comes down to managing deadlines proactively, and that is exactly what we do. You will always know what is due and when, with reminders and submissions handled for you.
Ready to take tax off your plate? Get in touch with our tax team or call +27 73 142 1010 to chat about how our tax services fit your business.

faqs.q: Do I need to register for VAT for my small business?
faqs.a: VAT registration in South Africa is compulsory once your taxable turnover exceeds R1 million in any 12-month period, and voluntary from R50 000. We assess your situation, handle the registration with SARS, and manage ongoing returns so you stay compliant from day one.

faqs.q: What is provisional tax and do I have to pay it?
faqs.a: Provisional tax spreads your income tax across the year via two payments, so you are not hit with one large bill. Most business owners and those with non-salary income qualify. We calculate your estimates carefully and submit on time to keep you penalty-free.

faqs.q: Can you help if SARS is auditing or querying my taxes?
faqs.a: Yes. We handle SARS correspondence, verification requests, audits and disputes on your behalf. As registered tax practitioners, we lodge objections, supply supporting documents and negotiate where needed, resolving issues quickly while protecting you from avoidable penalties and interest.

faqs.q: How can you legally reduce my tax bill?
faqs.a: We use tax planning grounded in your actual numbers, claiming every allowable deduction, structuring income sensibly and timing decisions well. Everything stays fully within SARS rules. The goal is keeping more cash in your business legitimately, never cutting corners that create risk later.

faqs.q: Do you offer tax services for businesses outside Cape Town?
faqs.a: Absolutely. While we are based in Cape Town and Stellenbosch, we serve SME owners across South Africa. Our work is cloud-based, so wherever you trade, we manage your income tax, provisional tax, VAT and SARS compliance remotely and securely.

payroll-services.metaTitle: Payroll Services Cape Town & Stellenbosch | Thryv
payroll-services.metaDescription: Outsource payroll to Thryv. Accurate monthly payroll, payslips, PAYE, UIF, SDL and SARS submissions for South African SMEs. Get your free payroll quote today.

body.heading: Payroll Services That Get Your Team Paid Right
paragraphs: Payroll is unforgiving. One error and your team is short-paid or SARS comes knocking. Our payroll services take the whole job off your hands, processing accurately each month and handling every SARS and Department of Labour submission so your people are paid correctly, on time, every time.

When you outsource payroll to us, you swap admin and stress for certainty. We manage payslips, statutory deductions and compliance in the background, freeing you to focus on growing your business across Cape Town, Stellenbosch and South Africa.
Payroll works best alongside clean financials, so it slots neatly into your monthly accounting services for a single, joined-up view of your numbers.

body.heading: PAYE, UIF, SDL and Every Submission Covered
paragraphs: Each month we calculate and submit your PAYE, UIF and SDL, file your EMP201 with SARS, and handle the twice-yearly EMP501 reconciliations that trip so many businesses up. Deadlines are managed for you, so penalties and late filings become a thing of the past.

We also take care of Department of Labour obligations, including UIF declarations and Workmen's Compensation (WCA) returns. Leave balances and statutory deductions are tracked accurately, keeping your records clean and ready for any inspection.
Because payroll feeds your tax position, we keep it aligned with your tax services, so there are no nasty surprises when annual returns come around.

body.heading: Accurate Payslips and Happy, Confident Staff
paragraphs: Every pay cycle, your employees receive clear, compliant payslips showing exactly what they have earned and what has been deducted. That transparency builds trust and saves you fielding payroll questions while you are trying to run the business.
From a single hire to a growing team, our payroll services in South Africa scale with you. Whether you employ two people or twenty, the process stays accurate, on time and fully compliant.
Want payroll handled properly from next month? Speak to our payroll team or call +27 73 142 1010 for a free, no-obligation quote.

faqs.q: What does outsourcing payroll to Thryv include?
faqs.a: We handle the full monthly cycle: processing salaries, issuing payslips, calculating PAYE, UIF and SDL, and submitting your EMP201 to SARS. We also manage Department of Labour returns, leave tracking and the EMP501 reconciliations, so your payroll is accurate and fully compliant.

faqs.q: What is the difference between EMP201 and EMP501?
faqs.a: The EMP201 is a monthly declaration of the PAYE, UIF and SDL you owe SARS. The EMP501 is a twice-yearly reconciliation matching those monthly payments to employee tax certificates. We prepare and submit both correctly and on time, so your records always reconcile.

faqs.q: Do you handle Department of Labour and UIF submissions?
faqs.a: Yes. Beyond SARS, we manage your Department of Labour obligations, including UIF declarations and Workmen's Compensation (WCA) returns. We keep these submissions accurate and current, so your business stays compliant and protected against penalties or disputes down the line.

faqs.q: Is outsourcing payroll worth it for a small team?
faqs.a: Definitely. Even with a few employees, payroll carries real compliance risk and eats valuable hours. Outsourcing payroll to us is cost-effective, removes the admin burden and guarantees accurate, on-time submissions, so a small mistake never turns into a costly SARS or Department of Labour problem.

faqs.q: Can you manage payroll for staff outside Cape Town?
faqs.a: Yes. We are based in Cape Town and Stellenbosch but provide payroll services across South Africa. Our cloud-based process means we can manage your team's pay, payslips and all statutory submissions accurately and securely, wherever your business and employees are located.

audit-services.metaTitle: Audit Services | Auditors in Cape Town | Thryv
audit-services.metaDescription: Trusted audit services and auditors in Cape Town. Thryv delivers statutory and external audits that satisfy the Companies Act. Talk to our audit firm today.

body.heading: Audit services that give your numbers credibility
paragraphs: Our audit services give you the highest level of assurance over your financial statements, so investors, lenders and regulators can trust your numbers without second-guessing. Whether the Companies Act requires it or your board simply wants peace of mind, we tailor each engagement to your business.

As experienced auditors in Cape Town, we handle both statutory and voluntary audits across a range of SME sectors. You get a clear, defensible opinion backed by thorough testing, not a tick-box exercise. We explain what every finding means for you in plain language.
Not sure whether you need a full audit? If your Public Interest Score is lower, an independent review may be a cost-effective alternative worth discussing first.

body.heading: What our audit engagement covers
paragraphs: Every external audit in South Africa starts with risk assessment and careful planning. We get to know your business, identify where things could go wrong, and design our testing around the areas that matter most, so the work is focused and efficient.

From there we carry out substantive testing of balances and transactions, verifying that your records reflect reality. We follow International Standards on Auditing throughout, giving your stakeholders confidence that the statutory audit was done properly and independently.
If your annual financial statements still need preparing, we can compile those first and then audit a clean, well-presented set of accounts.

body.heading: Clear reports and practical control guidance
paragraphs: You receive a clear audit report setting out our opinion in language you can act on, not pages of impenetrable jargon. We walk you through it personally so you understand exactly what it says about your business and why.

Beyond the opinion, a good audit firm adds value year-round. We flag weaknesses in your internal controls and give you practical guidance to tighten them, reducing fraud risk and making next year's audit smoother.
Ready to get started? Book a chat with our Cape Town audit team and we will scope an engagement that fits your business and budget.

faqs.q: Does my company legally need an audit?
faqs.a: It depends on your Public Interest Score and how your financial statements are compiled. Public companies and certain others must be audited under the Companies Act, while many SMEs qualify for an independent review instead. We assess your score and confirm exactly what you need before any work begins.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a statutory and a voluntary audit?
faqs.a: A statutory audit is one the Companies Act or your Memorandum of Incorporation requires by law. A voluntary audit is one you choose to have, often because a bank, investor or shareholder wants extra assurance. Both follow the same rigorous standards and deliver the same level of confidence in your numbers.

faqs.q: How long does an audit take?
faqs.a: Timing depends on the size of your business, the quality of your records and how quickly information is provided. A typical SME audit runs a few weeks from fieldwork to final report. Good preparation and well-organised records shorten the process considerably, and we give you a clear timeline upfront.

faqs.q: Are you registered auditors in Cape Town?
faqs.a: Yes. Thryv works with registered auditors serving clients across Cape Town, Stellenbosch and the wider Western Cape. We comply fully with IRBA requirements and International Standards on Auditing, so your audit holds up to scrutiny from regulators, lenders and investors alike.

faqs.q: What should I prepare before the audit starts?
faqs.a: Have your trial balance, bank statements, invoices, contracts and prior-year financials ready and organised. The cleaner your records, the faster and more cost-effective the audit. We send you a tailored checklist beforehand and are happy to help with bookkeeping or financial statement preparation first if needed.

independent-review.metaTitle: Independent Review Services | Companies Act | Thryv
independent-review.metaDescription: Need an independent review? Thryv offers a cost-effective audit alternative that meets Companies Act requirements. Check your Public Interest Score with us today.

body.heading: A cost-effective alternative to a full audit
paragraphs: For companies that do not require a full audit, an independent review is a cost-effective way to satisfy the Companies Act and reassure your stakeholders. It gives limited assurance over your financial statements at a fraction of the time and cost of an audit.

Many owner-managed SMEs in South Africa qualify for this lighter-touch option. As your independent reviewer, we confirm that your financial statements are credible and free of obvious material misstatement, giving banks and shareholders the comfort they need.
If it turns out a full audit is required, our audit services are ready to step in, so you are covered either way.

body.heading: Checking your Public Interest Score
paragraphs: Whether you need a review or an audit hinges on your Public Interest Score, a calculation set out in the Companies Act based on turnover, employees, third-party liabilities and shareholders. Getting it right matters, because the wrong choice means wasted money or non-compliance.

We assess your Public Interest Score accurately and explain which independent review requirements apply to your company. There is no guesswork, you get a clear answer on exactly what the law expects of you this financial year.
Once we have confirmed your obligations, we can also prepare your financial statements ready for review, keeping everything under one roof.

body.heading: Engagement, reporting and compliance
paragraphs: Our independent review engagement follows the International Standard on Review Engagements, applying enquiry and analytical procedures to your financials. We then issue a limited-assurance review report you can hand to lenders, investors and the CIPC with confidence.

Should we come across a reportable irregularity during the review, we guide you through your obligations under the Companies Act calmly and clearly. You are never left to navigate a compliance issue on your own.
Want to know which path applies to your business? Get in touch with our team and we will check your score and map out the next steps.

faqs.q: What is an independent review?
faqs.a: An independent review is a limited-assurance engagement where a qualified independent reviewer examines your financial statements using enquiry and analytical procedures. It offers less assurance than an audit but satisfies the Companies Act for many SMEs, making it a faster, more affordable way to keep stakeholders confident in your numbers.

faqs.q: How do I know if I qualify for an independent review?
faqs.a: It comes down to your Public Interest Score and who prepares your financial statements. Companies with a lower score that are not owner-managed typically qualify for a review rather than a full audit. We calculate your score precisely and confirm whether an independent review meets your Companies Act obligations.

faqs.q: What is the difference between an audit and an independent review?
faqs.a: An audit provides reasonable, high-level assurance through detailed testing of your records. An independent review provides limited assurance using enquiry and analytical procedures, so it is quicker and more affordable. The right choice depends on your Public Interest Score and what your stakeholders or the Companies Act require.

faqs.q: What is a Public Interest Score?
faqs.a: The Public Interest Score is a Companies Act calculation based on your average number of employees, third-party liabilities, turnover and number of shareholders. It determines whether your company needs an audit, an independent review or neither. We work it out for you and explain what it means for your reporting duties.

faqs.q: What happens if a reportable irregularity is found?
faqs.a: If your independent reviewer identifies a reportable irregularity, there are specific reporting obligations under the Companies Act. We handle this carefully, explaining the situation, the required steps and the timelines in plain language. Our goal is to protect your business while keeping you fully compliant with South African law.

financial-statement-preparation.metaDescription: Need professionally prepared annual financial statements in South Africa? Thryv delivers IFRS-compliant, funder-ready AFS. Talk to our chartered accountants today.

body.heading: What goes into your annual financial statements
paragraphs: Your annual financial statements are more than a compliance tick-box. We prepare a full set, including your income statement, balance sheet and cash flow statement, plus a statement of changes in equity. Together these show exactly how your business earns, spends and grows, giving you a clear, honest picture you can actually use.

Every set comes with the notes and disclosures that lenders, investors and SARS expect to see. We make sure the numbers tell a consistent story, so your financial statements stand up to scrutiny and you feel confident putting your name behind them.
Solid financial statements start with clean records, which is why AFS preparation works best alongside ongoing accounting services that keep your books accurate all year round.

body.heading: IFRS for SMEs compliance, done properly
paragraphs: South African companies are expected to report on a recognised framework, and for most SMEs that means IFRS for SMEs. We prepare your statements to the correct standard, applying the right recognition, measurement and disclosure rules so your reporting is credible and compliant.

Getting the framework right matters when a bank, the CIPC or a potential buyer reviews your numbers. We handle the technical detail, from accounting policies to disclosure notes, so nothing trips you up later.
If your company also requires assurance, well-prepared statements feed neatly into an independent review or full audit, saving you time and cost down the line.

body.heading: Director and stakeholder-ready reporting
paragraphs: Good financial statements help you make better decisions. We prepare director and stakeholder-ready reporting that highlights what matters, so board meetings, funding applications and shareholder updates are backed by numbers everyone can trust.

Whether you are approaching a bank for finance, bringing on an investor or simply planning the year ahead, clear AFS give your conversations weight. You walk in prepared, not scrambling for figures at the last minute.
Ready to turn your financial data into a real advantage? Get in touch with our team and we will prepare statements your business can rely on.

faqs.q: What are annual financial statements?
faqs.a: Annual financial statements are a formal record of your company's financial performance and position over a year. A complete set includes the income statement, balance sheet, cash flow statement, statement of changes in equity, plus supporting notes and disclosures prepared on a recognised framework like IFRS for SMEs.

faqs.q: Are South African companies legally required to prepare AFS?
faqs.a: Yes. Under the Companies Act, every South African company must prepare annual financial statements within six months of its financial year-end. The level of assurance required, whether an audit or independent review, depends on your company's public interest score and how the statements are compiled.

faqs.q: What is IFRS for SMEs and does it apply to me?
faqs.a: IFRS for SMEs is a simplified reporting framework designed for small and medium businesses that do not have public accountability. Most owner-managed South African companies report on it because it is less onerous than full IFRS while still being credible to banks, SARS and investors.

faqs.q: Can well-prepared financial statements help me get funding?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Banks and investors rely on your AFS to judge whether your business is viable and creditworthy. Clear, compliant, professionally prepared financial statements make funding conversations far smoother and signal that your business is well run and worth backing.

faqs.q: How long does AFS preparation take?
faqs.a: It depends on how complete and accurate your records are. With well-maintained books, a set of annual financial statements can often be ready within a few weeks. Disorganised records take longer, which is why ongoing accounting support throughout the year makes year-end far quicker.

company-secretary.metaTitle: Company Secretary Services | Thryv CIPC Compliance
company-secretary.metaDescription: Stay CIPC compliant with Thryv's company secretary services in South Africa. We handle annual returns, beneficial ownership and statutory registers. Contact us today.

body.heading: Keep your company CIPC compliant, year after year
paragraphs: Staying compliant with the CIPC is non-negotiable, but it eats into time you would rather spend running the business. Our company secretary services take that load off your plate, managing your statutory obligations quietly in the background so deadlines never catch you out.

We handle your CIPC annual returns on time, every time, helping you avoid penalties and the risk of your company being deregistered. You get peace of mind knowing the admin is handled and your company stays in good standing.
Corporate secretarial work sits naturally alongside your reporting, so many clients pair it with annual financial statement preparation for a single, joined-up compliance service.

body.heading: Statutory registers and company changes
paragraphs: Every company must keep accurate statutory registers, recording directors, shareholders and key company details. We set these up correctly and keep them current, so your records are always ready if the CIPC, a bank or a buyer asks to see them.

Business changes over time, and so must your records. We handle company registration, amendments to your Memorandum of Incorporation, and director and shareholder changes, filing everything with the CIPC accurately and on time.
Because your statutory records feed into your wider financial picture, our company secretary work fits neatly with your day-to-day accounting for one clear view of your business.

body.heading: Beneficial ownership filings handled for you
paragraphs: The CIPC now requires companies to file beneficial ownership information, disclosing who ultimately owns and controls the business. It is a relatively new obligation, and getting it wrong carries real compliance risk.

We prepare and submit your beneficial ownership filings, keeping the supporting register accurate and up to date. You stay on the right side of the rules without having to decode the requirements yourself.
Not sure whether your company is fully compliant? Speak to our team and we will review your statutory standing and take the pressure off your shoulders.

faqs.q: What does a company secretary actually do?
faqs.a: A company secretary manages your business's statutory and CIPC compliance obligations. That includes filing annual returns, maintaining statutory registers, recording director and shareholder changes, submitting beneficial ownership filings and keeping your company in good legal standing under the Companies Act.

faqs.q: What are CIPC annual returns and when are they due?
faqs.a: CIPC annual returns are a yearly filing confirming your company is still active and its details are current. They are due each year within 30 business days of your company's registration anniversary. Missing them risks penalties and eventual deregistration of your company.

faqs.q: What are beneficial ownership filings?
faqs.a: Beneficial ownership filings disclose the natural persons who ultimately own or control your company. The CIPC requires this information to improve transparency and combat financial crime. Companies must file it and keep an accurate beneficial ownership register, updating it whenever ownership details change.

faqs.q: What happens if I miss CIPC compliance deadlines?
faqs.a: Missing deadlines can lead to penalties, your company being placed in deregistration and, ultimately, removal from the register. That can freeze bank accounts and contracts. Using a company secretary service keeps every filing on track so your business stays compliant and operational.

faqs.q: Do small businesses need company secretary services?
faqs.a: Yes. Every registered company, regardless of size, has CIPC and statutory obligations to meet. Smaller businesses often benefit most, because owners rarely have time to track every deadline. Outsourcing the work keeps you compliant without adding to your already full workload.

finance-consultancy-services.metaTitle: Financial Consultancy for SMEs | Thryv South Africa
finance-consultancy-services.metaDescription: Practical financial consultancy for South African SMEs. Forecasting, management accounts and outsourced CFO support to help you grow. Book a free chat today.

body.heading: Financial consultancy that helps you plan and grow
paragraphs: Good numbers tell you where you are. Great financial consultancy tells you where you could be. We work alongside you as your business accountant and sounding board, turning your figures into clear, practical decisions. No jargon, no guesswork, just straight answers you can act on.

Whether you are tightening costs, chasing a new opportunity or planning your next year, we help you see the full picture. Think of us as outsourced CFO support without the full-time salary, giving Cape Town and Stellenbosch business owners the senior financial thinking usually reserved for bigger companies.
If you want the day-to-day basics handled too, our accounting services keep your books accurate so every decision rests on solid figures.

body.heading: Cash flow forecasting and management accounts you can trust
paragraphs: Cash is what keeps the doors open, so we put cash flow forecasting at the heart of what we do. We map out the months ahead, flag the tight spots early and help you plan around them, so you are never caught off guard by a quiet season or a big payment.

Our management accounts and KPI reporting give you a clear monthly view of how the business is really performing. We highlight the numbers that matter, explain what they mean in plain language, and help you spot trends before they become problems.
Together with budgeting and profitability analysis, this gives you a steady rhythm of insight, so you always know which products, clients or services are truly pulling their weight.

body.heading: Funding readiness and business advisory in South Africa
paragraphs: When you are ready to raise finance or attract investment, presentation matters. We get your numbers funding-ready, building the forecasts, business plans and scenario models that lenders and investors expect to see. You walk in prepared and confident.

Our business advisory in South Africa goes beyond the spreadsheet. We help you model different scenarios, weigh up big decisions and plan growth at a pace your cash flow can handle, so ambition and reality stay in step.
Ready to put your numbers to work? Get in touch for a relaxed, no-pressure chat about where you want to take your business.

faqs.q: What does a financial consultant actually do?
faqs.a: A financial consultant helps you understand your numbers and use them to make better decisions. At Thryv that means cash flow forecasting, management accounts, budgeting, profitability analysis and business advisory, all explained in plain language so you can plan, fund and grow your business with real confidence.

faqs.q: How is financial consultancy different from accounting?
faqs.a: Accounting records what has already happened and keeps you compliant. Financial consultancy looks forward, using those numbers to plan, forecast and improve performance. Most clients use both together, so the books stay accurate while we help you turn the figures into a clear strategy for growth.

faqs.q: Do I need an outsourced CFO if I am a small business?
faqs.a: Many SMEs benefit from senior financial thinking long before they can justify a full-time CFO salary. Outsourced CFO support gives you that expertise on a flexible, affordable basis, helping with forecasting, funding and big decisions, so you get the guidance of a finance director without the full-time cost.

faqs.q: Can you help me get my business ready for funding?
faqs.a: Yes. We prepare the forecasts, business plans and scenario models that banks and investors expect, and help you present your numbers clearly and credibly. Whether you are applying for a loan or seeking investment, we make sure your financials tell a strong, honest story that supports your case.

faqs.q: Do you offer financial consultancy outside Cape Town and Stellenbosch?
faqs.a: Yes. While we are based in Cape Town and Stellenbosch, we work with SME owners across South Africa. Most of our consultancy happens remotely through video calls and shared reports, so you get the same hands-on support and clear advice wherever your business is based.

employer-of-record.metaTitle: Employer of Record South Africa | EOR | Thryv
employer-of-record.metaDescription: Hire employees in South Africa without a local entity. Thryv is your compliant Employer of Record, handling payroll, tax and HR. Talk to us today.

body.heading: Employer of Record in South Africa, made simple
paragraphs: Want to hire employees in South Africa without the cost and delay of registering a local company? As your employer of record, we legally employ your team on your behalf, while they work day-to-day for you. You get the talent; we handle the paperwork.

Setting up a local entity can take months and tie you to ongoing admin you may not need. Our EOR South Africa service gets your people working compliantly in weeks, not quarters, so you can move quickly and test the market with confidence.

Often compared to a PEO model, our employer of record solution carries the full legal employment responsibility, giving overseas and local businesses a clean, low-risk way to build a South African team.

body.heading: Payroll, tax and statutory compliance handled
paragraphs: We take care of payroll outsourcing from start to finish, paying your team accurately and on time, every time. PAYE, UIF and SDL submissions to SARS are all managed for you, so nothing slips through the cracks and no deadlines are missed.

Beyond payroll, we handle tax, benefits administration and all the statutory submissions that come with employing people in South Africa. If you would like the same precision for an existing local team, our dedicated payroll services cover that too.
You get one clear point of contact and one tidy monthly process, instead of juggling multiple providers and unfamiliar local rules.

body.heading: Compliant contracts, onboarding and labour law
paragraphs: Every hire starts with a compliant local employment contract that meets South African labour law, including the Basic Conditions of Employment Act. We get the terms right from day one, protecting both you and your new team member.

We manage onboarding and HR compliance so your people feel welcomed and properly set up, while you stay on the right side of regulations. As laws change, we keep your employment arrangements up to date, so ongoing compliance is never something you have to chase.
Planning to expand into South Africa? Speak to our team and we will map out the simplest, fastest route to getting your people on the ground.

faqs.q: What is an Employer of Record in South Africa?
faqs.a: An employer of record (EOR) is a company that legally employs staff on your behalf in South Africa, while they work day-to-day for you. We handle contracts, payroll, tax and compliance, so you can hire employees in South Africa quickly without setting up your own local entity.

faqs.q: How is an EOR different from a PEO?
faqs.a: A PEO co-employs staff alongside your existing local entity, while an EOR employs them fully on your behalf, so you do not need an entity at all. For most businesses expanding into South Africa for the first time, the EOR model is simpler, faster and carries far less setup and compliance risk.

faqs.q: How quickly can I hire someone through your EOR service?
faqs.a: In most cases we can have a new employee compliantly contracted and onboarded within a couple of weeks, rather than the months it takes to register a local company. Once your details are confirmed, we prepare the contract, set up payroll and handle the statutory registrations on your behalf.

faqs.q: Which taxes and statutory costs do you handle?
faqs.a: We manage PAYE income tax, UIF and SDL submissions to SARS, along with any required benefits administration. Everything is calculated, deducted and submitted on time as part of our employer of record service, so your South African team stays fully compliant and you avoid penalties and missed deadlines.

faqs.q: Do you only help overseas companies expand into South Africa?
faqs.a: No. We support both international businesses entering the South African market and local companies that want to outsource employment, payroll and HR compliance. Whether you are testing a new region or simply want to hand over the admin, our EOR service scales to suit how you want to grow.
